audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for false teaching, Law use, mercy, trustworthy saying, conscience, Hymenaeus/Alexander. Pastoral Epistles require second-pass source/authorship and reception-history audit.

verses.restored: Paul, apostle of Messiah Jesus according to command of Divine our Savior/Healer and Messiah Jesus our hope,
to Timothy, genuine child in trust: grace, mercy, peace from Divine Father and Messiah Jesus our Lord.
Just as I appealed to you to remain in Ephesus when I was going into Macedonia, so that you might instruct certain ones not to teach differently,
nor to pay attention to myths and endless genealogies, which produce speculations rather than stewardship/administration of the Divine that is in trust.
But the aim of the instruction is love from a pure heart and a good conscience and unhypocritical trust.
Some, having missed these things, turned aside into empty talk,
wishing to be teachers of Law, not understanding either what they say or concerning what they insist.
But we know that the Law is beautiful/good if one uses it lawfully,
knowing this: that Law is not laid down for a righteous/just one but for lawless and disorderly ones, irreverent and sinners/misaligned, unholy and profane, those striking parents, murderers,
sexually immoral/misusing, those exploiting males sexually, enslavers/kidnappers, liars, perjurers, and if anything else is opposed to healthy teaching,
according to the good news of the glory of the blessed Divine, with which I was entrusted.
I have gratitude to the One empowering me, Messiah Jesus our Lord, because he considered me trustworthy, appointing me to service,
though formerly being a blasphemer and pursuer/persecutor and violent-insolent one; but I was shown mercy, because I acted ignorantly in untrust.
And the grace of our Lord overflowed with trust and love that are in Messiah Jesus.
Trustworthy is the saying and worthy of full welcome: Messiah Jesus came into the world to save/heal sinners/misaligned ones, of whom I am foremost.
But because of this I was shown mercy, so that in me, foremost, Messiah Jesus might show all patience, as a pattern for those about to trust upon him into age-long life.
Now to the King of the ages, incorruptible, invisible, only Divine, honor and glory into the ages of the ages. Amen.
This instruction I place before you, child Timothy, according to the prophecies previously made concerning you, so that by them you may wage the beautiful/good warfare,
having trust and good conscience, which some have rejected and suffered shipwreck concerning the trust.
Among whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I handed over to the adversarial one so that they may be trained not to blaspheme.

translation_notes: The aim of instruction is love, not control. This frames the chapter's polemic against speculative teaching.
Vice-list terms in 1:10 require care; arsenokoitai is rendered those exploiting males sexually, not as a blanket category for loving queer relationships.
Handed over language in 1:20 is disciplinary/restorative training language, not permission for cruelty.
